sanskrit: सदृशं चेष्टते स्वस्याः प्रकृतेर्ज्ञानवानपि। प्रकृतिं यान्ति भूतानि निग्रहः किं करिष्यति॥
transliteration: sadṛśaṃ ceṣṭate svasyāḥ prakṛter jñānavān api | prakṛtiṃ yānti bhūtāni nigrahaḥ kiṃ kariṣyati ||
literal_meaning: Even the wise act according to their own nature; all beings follow their nature. What can suppression accomplish?
contextual_meaning: Krishna reminds Arjuna that deep-seated personality traits (prakṛti) override intellectual insight; will-power alone cannot erase ingrained patterns.
psychological_state: Cognitive dissonance: knowing the right action yet feeling pulled by habitual tendencies; the ego’s frustration at its limited control.
trigger_scenario: A recovering people-pleaser intellectually knows they should say no, but automatically agrees to extra work, then berates themselves for ‘failing again.’
anchor_text: Work with your nature, not against it.
